2606.27005.title: Adaptive Utility driven Resource Orchestration for Resilient AI (AURORA-AI)
2606.27005.paper_summary: Modern AI systems are increasingly deployed under non-stationary computational, demographic, and operational conditions in which static resource allocation strategies degrade both predictive performance and human-centric properties such as fairness and explainability. This paper presents AURORA-AI, an Adaptive Utility-driven Resource Orchestration framework for Resilient AI that unifies Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman feedback control, Lyapunov-based stability monitoring, and a fairness-aware composite utility into a single closed-loop policy.The framework continuously redistributes computational budget across a population of heterogeneous AI models so that the global utility, defined jointly over predictive performance, demographic parity, cost, latency, robustness, and interpretability, remains maximised under disruption. The framework is evaluated in a stress-rich discrete-time simulation that concurrently injects demographic bias shocks, gradual concept drift, and abrupt black-swan disruptions, and is compared against five established controllers including Static, Round Robin, Greedy, LinUCB, and a deep reinforcement-learning agent based on Proximal Policy Optimisation. AURORA-AI achieves immediate recovery from the black-swan event compared to eighty-eight time steps for the Static baseline and twenty-two for Proximal Policy Optimisation, lifts the alpha-quantile and the super-quantile by twenty-nine and twenty-five percent respectively, simultaneously reduces the mean and maximum demographic parity gap, and increases the fraction of Lyapunov-stable operating steps. These results indicate that fairness-aware adaptive orchestration grounded in stability theory is a practical and theoretically motivated path toward resilient human-centric AI deployment.
2606.27005.paper_summary_zh: 現代的人工智慧系統越來越多地在非靜態的計算、人口統計和操作條件下部署，在這些條件下，靜態資源分配策略會降低預測性能以及以人為中心的特性，如公平性和可解釋性。本文提出了AURORA-AI，一個以適應性效用驅動的資源編排框架，用於韌性AI，該框架將Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman反饋控制、基於Lyapunov的穩定性監測和公平性意識的綜合效用統一為一個閉環策略。該框架持續在一群異質AI模型中重新分配計算預算，以便在中斷情況下，定義在預測性能、人口統計平等、成本、延遲、穩健性和可解釋性上的全球效用保持最大化。該框架在一個壓力豐富的離散時間模擬中進行評估，該模擬同時注入人口統計偏見衝擊、逐漸的概念漂移和突發的黑天鵝中斷，並與五個已建立的控制器進行比較，包括靜態、輪詢、貪婪、LinUCB，以及基於近端策略優化的深度強化學習代理。與靜態基準的八十八個時間步和近端策略優化的二十二個時間步相比，AURORA-AI實現了對黑天鵝事件的立即恢復，分別提升了alpha分位數和超分位數二十九和二十五個百分點，同時減少了平均和最大的人口統計平等差距，並增加了Lyapunov穩定操作步驟的比例。這些結果表明，基於穩定性理論的公平性意識適應性編排是一條實用且理論上有動機的道路，朝向韌性的人為中心AI部署。
2606.27005.author: Rahul Umesh Mhapsekar et.al.
2606.27005.authors: Rahul Umesh Mhapsekar, Ilias Cherkaoui, Lizy Abraham, Indrakshi Dey
